593	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
594	-- Vertical composition @$$@
595	
596	-- | Above, except that if the last line of the first argument stops
597	-- at least one position before the first line of the second begins,
598	-- these two lines are overlapped.  For example:
599	--
600	-- >    text "hi" $$ nest 5 (text "there")
601	--
602	-- lays out as
603	--
604	-- >    hi   there
605	--
606	-- rather than
607	--
608	-- >    hi
609	-- >         there
610	--
611	-- '$$' is associative, with identity 'empty', and also satisfies
612	--
613	-- * @(x '$$' y) '<>' z = x '$$' (y '<>' z)@, if @y@ non-empty.
614	--
615	($$) :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
616	p $$  q = above_ p False q
617	
618	-- | Above, with no overlapping.
619	-- '$+$' is associative, with identity 'empty'.
620	($+$) :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
621	p $+$ q = above_ p True q
622	
623	above_ :: Doc a -> Bool -> Doc a -> Doc a
624	above_ p _ Empty = p
625	above_ Empty _ q = q
626	above_ p g q     = Above p g q
627	
628	above :: Doc a -> Bool -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
629	above (Above p g1 q1)  g2 q2 = above p g1 (above q1 g2 q2)
630	above p@(Beside{})     g  q  = aboveNest (reduceDoc p) g 0 (reduceDoc q)
631	above p g q                  = aboveNest p             g 0 (reduceDoc q)
632	
633	-- Specfication: aboveNest p g k q = p $g$ (nest k q)
634	aboveNest :: RDoc a -> Bool -> Int -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
635	aboveNest _                   _ k _ | k `seq` False = undefined
636	aboveNest NoDoc               _ _ _ = NoDoc
637	aboveNest (p1 `Union` p2)     g k q = aboveNest p1 g k q `union_`
638	                                      aboveNest p2 g k q
639	
640	aboveNest Empty               _ k q = mkNest k q
641	aboveNest (Nest k1 p)         g k q = nest_ k1 (aboveNest p g (k - k1) q)
642	                                  -- p can't be Empty, so no need for mkNest
643	
644	aboveNest (NilAbove p)        g k q = nilAbove_ (aboveNest p g k q)
645	aboveNest (TextBeside s p)    g k q = TextBeside s rest
646	                                    where
647	                                      !k1  = k - annotSize s
648	                                      rest = case p of
649	                                                Empty -> nilAboveNest g k1 q
650	                                                _     -> aboveNest  p g k1 q
651	
652	aboveNest (Above {})          _ _ _ = error "aboveNest Above"
653	aboveNest (Beside {})         _ _ _ = error "aboveNest Beside"
654	
655	-- Specification: text s <> nilaboveNest g k q
656	--              = text s <> (text "" $g$ nest k q)
657	nilAboveNest :: Bool -> Int -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
658	nilAboveNest _ k _           | k `seq` False = undefined
659	nilAboveNest _ _ Empty       = Empty
660	                               -- Here's why the "text s <>" is in the spec!
661	nilAboveNest g k (Nest k1 q) = nilAboveNest g (k + k1) q
662	nilAboveNest g k q           | not g && k > 0      -- No newline if no overlap
663	                             = textBeside_ (NoAnnot (Str (indent k)) k) q
664	                             | otherwise           -- Put them really above
665	                             = nilAbove_ (mkNest k q)
666	
667	
668	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
669	-- Horizontal composition @<>@
670	
671	-- We intentionally avoid Data.Monoid.(<>) here due to interactions of
672	-- Data.Monoid.(<>) and (<+>).  See
673	-- http://www.haskell.org/pipermail/libraries/2011-November/017066.html
674	
675	-- | Beside.
676	-- '<>' is associative, with identity 'empty'.
677	(<>) :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
678	p <>  q = beside_ p False q
679	
680	-- | Beside, separated by space, unless one of the arguments is 'empty'.
681	-- '<+>' is associative, with identity 'empty'.
682	(<+>) :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
683	p <+> q = beside_ p True  q
684	
685	beside_ :: Doc a -> Bool -> Doc a -> Doc a
686	beside_ p _ Empty = p
687	beside_ Empty _ q = q
688	beside_ p g q     = Beside p g q
689	
690	-- Specification: beside g p q = p <g> q
691	beside :: Doc a -> Bool -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
692	beside NoDoc               _ _   = NoDoc
693	beside (p1 `Union` p2)     g q   = beside p1 g q `union_` beside p2 g q
694	beside Empty               _ q   = q
695	beside (Nest k p)          g q   = nest_ k $! beside p g q
696	beside p@(Beside p1 g1 q1) g2 q2
697	         | g1 == g2              = beside p1 g1 $! beside q1 g2 q2
698	         | otherwise             = beside (reduceDoc p) g2 q2
699	beside p@(Above{})         g q   = let !d = reduceDoc p in beside d g q
700	beside (NilAbove p)        g q   = nilAbove_ $! beside p g q
701	beside (TextBeside t p)    g q   = TextBeside t rest
702	                               where
703	                                  rest = case p of
704	                                           Empty -> nilBeside g q
705	                                           _     -> beside p g q
706	
707	-- Specification: text "" <> nilBeside g p
708	--              = text "" <g> p
709	nilBeside :: Bool -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
710	nilBeside _ Empty         = Empty -- Hence the text "" in the spec
711	nilBeside g (Nest _ p)    = nilBeside g p
712	nilBeside g p | g         = textBeside_ spaceText p
713	              | otherwise = p

716	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
717	-- Separate, @sep@
718	
719	-- Specification: sep ps  = oneLiner (hsep ps)
720	--                         `union`
721	--                          vcat ps
722	
723	-- | Either 'hsep' or 'vcat'.
724	sep  :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
725	sep = sepX True   -- Separate with spaces
726	
727	-- | Either 'hcat' or 'vcat'.
728	cat :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
729	cat = sepX False  -- Don't
730	
731	sepX :: Bool -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
732	sepX _ []     = empty
733	sepX x (p:ps) = sep1 x (reduceDoc p) 0 ps
734	
735	
736	-- Specification: sep1 g k ys = sep (x : map (nest k) ys)
737	--                            = oneLiner (x <g> nest k (hsep ys))
738	--                              `union` x $$ nest k (vcat ys)
739	sep1 :: Bool -> RDoc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> RDoc a
740	sep1 _ _                   k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
741	sep1 _ NoDoc               _ _  = NoDoc
742	sep1 g (p `Union` q)       k ys = sep1 g p k ys `union_`
743	                                  aboveNest q False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys))
744	
745	sep1 g Empty               k ys = mkNest k (sepX g ys)
746	sep1 g (Nest n p)          k ys = nest_ n (sep1 g p (k - n) ys)
747	
748	sep1 _ (NilAbove p)        k ys = nilAbove_
749	                                  (aboveNest p False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys)))
750	sep1 g (TextBeside s p) k ys    = textBeside_ s (sepNB g p (k - annotSize s) ys)
751	sep1 _ (Above {})          _ _  = error "sep1 Above"
752	sep1 _ (Beside {})         _ _  = error "sep1 Beside"
753	
754	-- Specification: sepNB p k ys = sep1 (text "" <> p) k ys
755	-- Called when we have already found some text in the first item
756	-- We have to eat up nests
757	sepNB :: Bool -> Doc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
758	sepNB g (Nest _ p) k ys
759	  = sepNB g p k ys -- Never triggered, because of invariant (2)
760	sepNB g Empty k ys
761	  = oneLiner (nilBeside g (reduceDoc rest)) `mkUnion`
762	    -- XXX: TODO: PRETTY: Used to use True here (but GHC used False...)
763	    nilAboveNest False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys))
764	  where
765	    rest | g         = hsep ys
766	         | otherwise = hcat ys
767	sepNB g p k ys
768	  = sep1 g p k ys
769

771	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
772	-- @fill@
773	
774	-- | \"Paragraph fill\" version of 'cat'.
775	fcat :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
776	fcat = fill False
777	
778	-- | \"Paragraph fill\" version of 'sep'.
779	fsep :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
780	fsep = fill True
781	
782	-- Specification:
783	--
784	-- fill g docs = fillIndent 0 docs
785	--
786	-- fillIndent k [] = []
787	-- fillIndent k [p] = p
788	-- fillIndent k (p1:p2:ps) =
789	--    oneLiner p1 <g> fillIndent (k + length p1 + g ? 1 : 0)
790	--                               (remove_nests (oneLiner p2) : ps)
791	--     `Union`
792	--    (p1 $*$ nest (-k) (fillIndent 0 ps))
793	--
794	-- $*$ is defined for layouts (not Docs) as
795	-- layout1 $*$ layout2 | hasMoreThanOneLine layout1 = layout1 $$ layout2
796	--                     | otherwise                  = layout1 $+$ layout2
797	
798	fill :: Bool -> [Doc a] -> RDoc a
799	fill _ []     = empty
800	fill g (p:ps) = fill1 g (reduceDoc p) 0 ps
801	
802	fill1 :: Bool -> RDoc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
803	fill1 _ _                   k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
804	fill1 _ NoDoc               _ _  = NoDoc
805	fill1 g (p `Union` q)       k ys = fill1 g p k ys `union_`
806	                                   aboveNest q False k (fill g ys)
807	fill1 g Empty               k ys = mkNest k (fill g ys)
808	fill1 g (Nest n p)          k ys = nest_ n (fill1 g p (k - n) ys)
809	fill1 g (NilAbove p)        k ys = nilAbove_ (aboveNest p False k (fill g ys))
810	fill1 g (TextBeside s p)    k ys = textBeside_ s (fillNB g p (k - annotSize s) ys)
811	fill1 _ (Above {})          _ _  = error "fill1 Above"
812	fill1 _ (Beside {})         _ _  = error "fill1 Beside"
813	
814	fillNB :: Bool -> Doc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
815	fillNB _ _           k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
816	fillNB g (Nest _ p)  k ys   = fillNB g p k ys
817	                              -- Never triggered, because of invariant (2)
818	fillNB _ Empty _ []         = Empty
819	fillNB g Empty k (Empty:ys) = fillNB g Empty k ys
820	fillNB g Empty k (y:ys)     = fillNBE g k y ys
821	fillNB g p k ys             = fill1 g p k ys
822	
823	
824	fillNBE :: Bool -> Int -> Doc a -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
825	fillNBE g k y ys
826	  = nilBeside g (fill1 g ((elideNest . oneLiner . reduceDoc) y) k' ys)
827	    -- XXX: TODO: PRETTY: Used to use True here (but GHC used False...)
828	    `mkUnion` nilAboveNest False k (fill g (y:ys))
829	  where k' = if g then k - 1 else k
830	
831	elideNest :: Doc a -> Doc a
832	elideNest (Nest _ d) = d
833	elideNest d          = d

836	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
837	-- Selecting the best layout
838	
839	best :: Int   -- Line length.
840	     -> Int   -- Ribbon length.
841	     -> RDoc a
842	     -> RDoc a  -- No unions in here!.
843	best w0 r = get w0
844	  where
845	    get w _ | w == 0 && False = undefined
846	    get _ Empty               = Empty
847	    get _ NoDoc               = NoDoc
848	    get w (NilAbove p)        = nilAbove_ (get w p)
849	    get w (TextBeside s p)    = textBeside_ s (get1 w (annotSize s) p)
850	    get w (Nest k p)          = nest_ k (get (w - k) p)
851	    get w (p `Union` q)       = nicest w r (get w p) (get w q)
852	    get _ (Above {})          = error "best get Above"
853	    get _ (Beside {})         = error "best get Beside"
854	
855	    get1 w _ _ | w == 0 && False  = undefined
856	    get1 _ _  Empty               = Empty
857	    get1 _ _  NoDoc               = NoDoc
858	    get1 w sl (NilAbove p)        = nilAbove_ (get (w - sl) p)
859	    get1 w sl (TextBeside s p)    = textBeside_ s (get1 w (sl + annotSize s) p)
860	    get1 w sl (Nest _ p)          = get1 w sl p
861	    get1 w sl (p `Union` q)       = nicest1 w r sl (get1 w sl p)
862	                                                   (get1 w sl q)
863	    get1 _ _  (Above {})          = error "best get1 Above"
864	    get1 _ _  (Beside {})         = error "best get1 Beside"
865	
866	nicest :: Int -> Int -> Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
867	nicest !w !r = nicest1 w r 0
868	
869	nicest1 :: Int -> Int -> Int -> Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
870	nicest1 !w !r !sl p q | fits ((w `min` r) - sl) p = p
871	                      | otherwise                 = q
872	
873	fits :: Int  -- Space available
874	     -> Doc a
875	     -> Bool -- True if *first line* of Doc fits in space available
876	fits n _ | n < 0           = False
877	fits _ NoDoc               = False
878	fits _ Empty               = True
879	fits _ (NilAbove _)        = True
880	fits n (TextBeside s p)    = fits (n - annotSize s) p
881	fits _ (Above {})          = error "fits Above"
882	fits _ (Beside {})         = error "fits Beside"
883	fits _ (Union {})          = error "fits Union"
884	fits _ (Nest {})           = error "fits Nest"
885	
886	-- | @first@ returns its first argument if it is non-empty, otherwise its
887	-- second.
888	first :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
889	first p q | nonEmptySet p = p -- unused, because (get OneLineMode) is unused
890	          | otherwise     = q
891	
892	nonEmptySet :: Doc a -> Bool
893	nonEmptySet NoDoc              = False
894	nonEmptySet (_ `Union` _)      = True
895	nonEmptySet Empty              = True
896	nonEmptySet (NilAbove _)       = True
897	nonEmptySet (TextBeside _ p)   = nonEmptySet p
898	nonEmptySet (Nest _ p)         = nonEmptySet p
899	nonEmptySet (Above {})         = error "nonEmptySet Above"
900	nonEmptySet (Beside {})        = error "nonEmptySet Beside"
901	
902	-- @oneLiner@ returns the one-line members of the given set of @GDoc@s.
903	oneLiner :: Doc a -> Doc a
904	oneLiner NoDoc               = NoDoc
905	oneLiner Empty               = Empty
906	oneLiner (NilAbove _)        = NoDoc
907	oneLiner (TextBeside s p)    = textBeside_ s (oneLiner p)
908	oneLiner (Nest k p)          = nest_ k (oneLiner p)
909	oneLiner (p `Union` _)       = oneLiner p
910	oneLiner (Above {})          = error "oneLiner Above"
911	oneLiner (Beside {})         = error "oneLiner Beside"

914	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
915	-- Rendering
916	
917	-- | A rendering style. Allows us to specify constraints to choose among the
918	-- many different rendering options.
919	data Style
920	  = Style { mode           :: Mode
921	            -- ^ The rendering mode.
922	          , lineLength     :: Int
923	            -- ^ Maximum length of a line, in characters.
924	          , ribbonsPerLine :: Float
925	            -- ^ Ratio of line length to ribbon length. A ribbon refers to the
926	            -- characters on a line /excluding/ indentation. So a 'lineLength'
927	            -- of 100, with a 'ribbonsPerLine' of @2.0@ would only allow up to
928	            -- 50 characters of ribbon to be displayed on a line, while
929	            -- allowing it to be indented up to 50 characters.
930	          }
931	#if __GLASGOW_HASKELL__ >= 701
932	  deriving (Show, Eq, Generic)
933	#endif
934	
935	-- | The default style (@mode=PageMode, lineLength=100, ribbonsPerLine=1.5@).
936	style :: Style
937	style = Style { lineLength = 100, ribbonsPerLine = 1.5, mode = PageMode }
938	
939	-- | Rendering mode.
940	data Mode = PageMode    
941	            -- ^ Normal rendering ('lineLength' and 'ribbonsPerLine'
942	            -- respected').
943	          | ZigZagMode  
944	            -- ^ With zig-zag cuts.
945	          | LeftMode    
946	            -- ^ No indentation, infinitely long lines ('lineLength' ignored),
947	            -- but explicit new lines, i.e., @text "one" $$ text "two"@, are
948	            -- respected.
949	          | OneLineMode 
950	            -- ^ All on one line, 'lineLength' ignored and explicit new lines
951	            -- (@$$@) are turned into spaces.
952	#if __GLASGOW_HASKELL__ >= 701
953	          deriving (Show, Eq, Generic)
954	#endif
955	
956	-- | Render the @Doc@ to a String using the default @Style@ (see 'style').
957	render :: Doc a -> String
958	render = fullRender (mode style) (lineLength style) (ribbonsPerLine style)
959	                    txtPrinter ""
960	
961	-- | Render the @Doc@ to a String using the given @Style@.
962	renderStyle :: Style -> Doc a -> String
963	renderStyle s = fullRender (mode s) (lineLength s) (ribbonsPerLine s)
964	                txtPrinter ""
965	
966	-- | Default TextDetails printer.
967	txtPrinter :: TextDetails -> String -> String
968	txtPrinter (Chr c)   s  = c:s
969	txtPrinter (Str s1)  s2 = s1 ++ s2
970	txtPrinter (PStr s1) s2 = s1 ++ s2
971	
972	-- | The general rendering interface. Please refer to the @Style@ and @Mode@
973	-- types for a description of rendering mode, line length and ribbons.
974	fullRender :: Mode                    -- ^ Rendering mode.
975	           -> Int                     -- ^ Line length.
976	           -> Float                   -- ^ Ribbons per line.
977	           -> (TextDetails -> a -> a) -- ^ What to do with text.
978	           -> a                       -- ^ What to do at the end.
979	           -> Doc b                   -- ^ The document.
980	           -> a                       -- ^ Result.
981	fullRender m l r txt = fullRenderAnn m l r annTxt
982	  where
983	  annTxt (NoAnnot s _) = txt s
984	  annTxt _             = id
985	
986	-- | The general rendering interface, supporting annotations. Please refer to
987	-- the @Style@ and @Mode@ types for a description of rendering mode, line
988	-- length and ribbons.
989	fullRenderAnn :: Mode                       -- ^ Rendering mode.
990	              -> Int                        -- ^ Line length.
991	              -> Float                      -- ^ Ribbons per line.
992	              -> (AnnotDetails b -> a -> a) -- ^ What to do with text.
993	              -> a                          -- ^ What to do at the end.
994	              -> Doc b                      -- ^ The document.
995	              -> a                          -- ^ Result.
996	fullRenderAnn OneLineMode _ _ txt end doc
997	  = easyDisplay spaceText (\_ y -> y) txt end (reduceDoc doc)
998	fullRenderAnn LeftMode    _ _ txt end doc
999	  = easyDisplay nlText first txt end (reduceDoc doc)
1000	
1001	fullRenderAnn m lineLen ribbons txt rest doc
1002	  = display m lineLen ribbonLen txt rest doc'
1003	  where
1004	    doc' = best bestLineLen ribbonLen (reduceDoc doc)
1005	
1006	    bestLineLen, ribbonLen :: Int
1007	    ribbonLen   = round (fromIntegral lineLen / ribbons)
1008	    bestLineLen = case m of
1009	                      ZigZagMode -> maxBound
1010	                      _          -> lineLen
1011	
1012	easyDisplay :: AnnotDetails b
1013	             -> (Doc b -> Doc b -> Doc b)
1014	             -> (AnnotDetails b -> a -> a)
1015	             -> a
1016	             -> Doc b
1017	             -> a
1018	easyDisplay nlSpaceText choose txt end
1019	  = lay
1020	  where
1021	    lay NoDoc              = error "easyDisplay: NoDoc"
1022	    lay (Union p q)        = lay (choose p q)
1023	    lay (Nest _ p)         = lay p
1024	    lay Empty              = end
1025	    lay (NilAbove p)       = nlSpaceText `txt` lay p
1026	    lay (TextBeside s p)   = s `txt` lay p
1027	    lay (Above {})         = error "easyDisplay Above"
1028	    lay (Beside {})        = error "easyDisplay Beside"
1029	
1030	display :: Mode -> Int -> Int -> (AnnotDetails b -> a -> a) -> a -> Doc b -> a
1031	display m !page_width !ribbon_width txt end doc
1032	  = case page_width - ribbon_width of { gap_width ->
1033	    case gap_width `quot` 2 of { shift ->
1034	    let
1035	        lay k _            | k `seq` False = undefined
1036	        lay k (Nest k1 p)  = lay (k + k1) p
1037	        lay _ Empty        = end
1038	        lay k (NilAbove p) = nlText `txt` lay k p
1039	        lay k (TextBeside s p)
1040	            = case m of
1041	                    ZigZagMode |  k >= gap_width
1042	                               -> nlText `txt` (
1043	                                  NoAnnot (Str (replicate shift '/')) shift `txt` (
1044	                                  nlText `txt`
1045	                                  lay1 (k - shift) s p ))
1046	
1047	                               |  k < 0
1048	                               -> nlText `txt` (
1049	                                  NoAnnot (Str (replicate shift '\\')) shift `txt` (
1050	                                  nlText `txt`
1051	                                  lay1 (k + shift) s p ))
1052	
1053	                    _ -> lay1 k s p
1054	
1055	        lay _ (Above {})   = error "display lay Above"
1056	        lay _ (Beside {})  = error "display lay Beside"
1057	        lay _ NoDoc        = error "display lay NoDoc"
1058	        lay _ (Union {})   = error "display lay Union"
1059	
1060	        lay1 !k s p        = let !r = k + annotSize s
1061	                             in NoAnnot (Str (indent k)) k `txt` (s `txt` lay2 r p)
1062	
1063	        lay2 k _ | k `seq` False   = undefined
1064	        lay2 k (NilAbove p)        = nlText `txt` lay k p
1065	        lay2 k (TextBeside s p)    = s `txt` lay2 (k + annotSize s) p
1066	        lay2 k (Nest _ p)          = lay2 k p
1067	        lay2 _ Empty               = end
1068	        lay2 _ (Above {})          = error "display lay2 Above"
1069	        lay2 _ (Beside {})         = error "display lay2 Beside"
1070	        lay2 _ NoDoc               = error "display lay2 NoDoc"
1071	        lay2 _ (Union {})          = error "display lay2 Union"
1072	    in
1073	    lay 0 doc
1074	    }}
1075

1078	-- Rendering Annotations -------------------------------------------------------
1079	
1080	-- | A @Span@ represents the result of an annotation after a @Doc@ has been
1081	-- rendered, capturing where the annotation now starts and ends in the rendered
1082	-- output.
1083	data Span a = Span { spanStart      :: !Int
1084	                   , spanLength     :: !Int
1085	                   , spanAnnotation :: a
1086	                   } deriving (Show,Eq)
1087	
1088	instance Functor Span where
1089	  fmap f (Span x y a) = Span x y (f a)
1090	
1091	
1092	-- State required for generating document spans.
1093	data Spans a = Spans { sOffset :: !Int
1094	                       -- ^ Current offset from the end of the document.
1095	                     , sStack  :: [Int -> Span a]
1096	                       -- ^ Currently open spans.
1097	                     , sSpans  :: [Span a]
1098	                       -- ^ Collected annotation regions.
1099	                     , sOutput :: String
1100	                       -- ^ Collected output.
1101	                     }
1102	
1103	-- | Render an annotated @Doc@ to a String and list of annotations (see 'Span')
1104	-- using the default @Style@ (see 'style').
1105	renderSpans :: Doc ann -> (String,[Span ann])
1106	renderSpans  = finalize
1107	             . fullRenderAnn (mode style) (lineLength style) (ribbonsPerLine style)
1108	                  spanPrinter
1109	                  Spans { sOffset = 0, sStack = [], sSpans = [], sOutput = "" }
1110	  where
1111	
1112	  finalize (Spans size _ spans out) = (out, map adjust spans)
1113	    where
1114	    adjust s = s { spanStart = size - spanStart s }
1115	
1116	  mkSpan a end start = Span { spanStart      = start
1117	                            , spanLength     = start - end
1118	                              -- this seems wrong, but remember that it's
1119	                              -- working backwards at this point
1120	                            , spanAnnotation = a }
1121	
1122	  -- the document gets generated in reverse, which is why the starting
1123	  -- annotation ends the annotation.
1124	  spanPrinter AnnotStart s =
1125	    case sStack s of
1126	      sp : rest -> s { sSpans = sp (sOffset s) : sSpans s, sStack = rest }
1127	      _         -> error "renderSpans: stack underflow"
1128	
1129	  spanPrinter (AnnotEnd a) s =
1130	    s { sStack = mkSpan a (sOffset s) : sStack s }
1131	
1132	  spanPrinter (NoAnnot td l) s =
1133	    case td of
1134	      Chr  c -> s { sOutput = c  : sOutput s, sOffset = sOffset s + l }
1135	      Str  t -> s { sOutput = t ++ sOutput s, sOffset = sOffset s + l }
1136	      PStr t -> s { sOutput = t ++ sOutput s, sOffset = sOffset s + l }
1137	
1138	
1139	-- | Render out a String, interpreting the annotations as part of the resulting
1140	-- document.
1141	--
1142	-- /IMPORTANT/: the size of the annotation string does NOT figure into the
1143	-- layout of the document, so the document will lay out as though the
1144	-- annotations are not present.
1145	renderDecorated :: (ann -> String) -- ^ Starting an annotation.
1146	                -> (ann -> String) -- ^ Ending an annotation.
1147	                -> Doc ann -> String
1148	renderDecorated startAnn endAnn =
1149	  finalize . fullRenderAnn (mode style) (lineLength style) (ribbonsPerLine style)
1150	                 annPrinter
1151	                 ("", [])
1152	  where
1153	  annPrinter AnnotStart (rest,stack) =
1154	    case stack of
1155	      a : as -> (startAnn a ++ rest, as)
1156	      _      -> error "renderDecorated: stack underflow"
1157	
1158	  annPrinter (AnnotEnd a) (rest,stack) =
1159	    (endAnn a ++ rest, a : stack)
1160	
1161	  annPrinter (NoAnnot s _) (rest,stack) =
1162	    (txtPrinter s rest, stack)
1163	
1164	  finalize (str,_) = str
1165	
1166	
1167	-- | Render a document with annotations, by interpreting the start and end of
1168	-- the annotations, as well as the text details in the context of a monad.
1169	renderDecoratedM :: Monad m
1170	                 => (ann    -> m r) -- ^ Starting an annotation.
1171	                 -> (ann    -> m r) -- ^ Ending an annotation.
1172	                 -> (String -> m r) -- ^ Text formatting.
1173	                 -> m r             -- ^ Document end.
1174	                 -> Doc ann -> m r
1175	renderDecoratedM startAnn endAnn txt docEnd =
1176	  finalize . fullRenderAnn (mode style) (lineLength style) (ribbonsPerLine style)
1177	                 annPrinter
1178	                 (docEnd, [])
1179	  where
1180	  annPrinter AnnotStart (rest,stack) =
1181	    case stack of
1182	      a : as -> (startAnn a >> rest, as)
1183	      _      -> error "renderDecorated: stack underflow"
1184	
1185	  annPrinter (AnnotEnd a) (rest,stack) =
1186	    (endAnn a >> rest, a : stack)
1187	
1188	  annPrinter (NoAnnot td _) (rest,stack) =
1189	    case td of
1190	      Chr  c -> (txt [c] >> rest, stack)
1191	      Str  s -> (txt s   >> rest, stack)
1192	      PStr s -> (txt s   >> rest, stack)
1193	
1194	  finalize (m,_) = m

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Serve a .spthy in the HS interactive UI on a given port (--port= form).
export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
BIN="/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover"
case "$1" in
  start)
    d=$(mktemp -d); cp "$2" "$d/";

=== drive.py ===
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Autoprove each lemma of a served theory and fetch its proof-state graph DOT.
# Usage: drive.py <port> <lemma1> [lemma2 ...]   -> prints "LEMMA\t<dotpath>" lines
import sys, re, json, urllib.request, os
PORT=sys.argv[1]; LEMMAS=sys.argv[2:]
BASE=f"http://127.0.0.1:{PORT}"
OUT=os.environ.get("OUTDIR","/tmp/probe_dots"); os.makedirs(OUT,exist_ok=True)
def get(path, timeout=300):
    with urllib.request.urlopen(BASE+path, timeout=timeout) as r:
        return r.read().decode("utf-8","replace")
def latest_index():
    idx=get("/")
    ms=re.findall(r'/thy/trace/(\d+)/', idx)
    return max(int(x) for x in ms) if ms else 1
for L in LEMMAS:
    i=latest_index()
    # autoprove this lemma from the current latest theory
    try:
        r=get(f"/thy/trace/{i}/autoprove/idfs/0/False/proof/{L}")
    except Exception as e:
